几个月来，硅谷一直不太确定该怎么看待乔·拜登。
拜登选择哈里斯(Harris)担任副总统可能会带来硅谷的兴奋和金钱的充裕，而其他竞选伙伴则不会这样做。哈里斯几十年来一直与旧金山的精英们关系融洽。对于一个直到最近还在努力让最富有、最有权势的科技大亨兴奋的顶级人物来说，哈里斯将带来来自亿万富翁阶层的超级粉丝，他们将为民主党人的金库带来超级充电，特别是在接下来的几天里。尽管这让拜登更加依赖这些大捐赠者。
在政策上，这位加州参议员的当选给一直紧张地关注着该党极左翼崛起的科技行业带来了一些安慰。拜登在竞选期间没有将科技问题作为优先事项，这给他的政府将在多大程度上推行监管，甚至拆分科技巨头带来了不确定性。与哈里斯-一位与许多领先的科技高管关系密切的政策实用主义者-拜登发出了另一个信号，即他的政府不会转向伊丽莎白·沃伦(Elizabeth Warren)等人推动的政策。沃伦是副总统的短篇小说家，他没有选择谁想要拆分大型科技公司。
硅谷民主党顶级筹款人库珀·特博(Cooper Teboe)表示，在与他交谈的西海岸主要捐赠者中，约有三分之一的人一直在等待拜登将选择谁担任副总统，然后再决定是否在这个周期向民主党人投资数万美元。例如，如果拜登选择了沃伦，技术捐赠者可能会有顾虑。
“对于捐赠界来说，她是最安全的选择，”Teboe在谈到哈里斯时说。“她将成为加州和硅谷捐赠者群体的选择--他们担心科技、遣返和税收等问题--他们会最高兴地选择她。”
根据Recode看到的一份邀请函，哈里斯与这一权力集团的关系将在几天后突显出来，届时她将与旧金山湾区的一个筹款团体一起主持一场高额筹款活动，名为选举女性湾区(Electing Women Bay Area)。
哈里斯与超级富豪的特殊接触一直是她在旧金山政治崛起不可或缺的一部分，在她作为司法部长和美国参议员赢得全州胜利之前，她首先在旧金山担任地区检察官。哈里斯是该市鸡尾酒巡回演出的常客，从30多岁起就在社会页面上发表个人简介。她的竞选活动得到了现代科技繁荣之前的老富豪家庭的资助。
当这股热潮真的到来时，哈里斯利用并建立了一个新钱粉丝的轨道，她将进一步将这些粉丝带入拜登的圈子。她在过去20年里最大的捐赠者读起来就像是一份科技大亨的名人榜：Salesforce创始人马克·贝尼奥夫告诉Recode，哈里斯是“我见过的最正直的人之一”。Facebook早期的总裁肖恩·帕克邀请哈里斯参加他的婚礼。她竞选总统的筹款人包括亿万富翁民主党权力掮客，如里德·霍夫曼(Reid Hoffman)和约翰·多尔(John Doerr)。
克里斯·勒汉(Chris Lehane)是旧金山湾区捐赠者的长期顾问，她回忆起哈里斯在2009年第一次竞选加州司法部长期间打筹款电话时是一匹“主力”。
“她会看完整个名单，”他说，“然后再问更多的名字。”
哈里斯与民主党超级捐赠者劳伦·鲍威尔·乔布斯(Laurene Powell Jobs)的关系特别密切，她是亿万富翁慈善家，也是已故史蒂夫·乔布斯(Steve Jobs)的妻子。2017年，当鲍威尔·乔布斯在一年一度的代码大会上登台发言时，她带来了哈里斯。
鲍威尔·乔布斯(Powell Jobs)在舞台上说，“我想你会发现这比只有她自己更有趣。”周二，她在推特上表示，拜登“做出了一个伟大的选择！”
但事实证明，所有这些联系对民主党来说都是双刃剑，因为民主党越来越关注这些亿万富翁积累的财富和他们的政治影响力。他们的科技公司也是如此，这些公司现在是反垄断审查的对象，也是对硅谷企业实力的更广泛反思的对象。
硅谷投资者罗杰·麦克纳米(Roger McNamee)曾对拜登过多听取科技亿万富翁的意见表示担忧，他表示，哈里斯可能会实现“尼克松对中国的时刻”。换句话说，只有像她这样的人才能推动某些法规的通过，因为她在科技界的信誉。
麦克纳米说：“作为加利福尼亚州的参议员，卡马拉·哈里斯与大型科技公司结盟是可以理解的。”“作为副总统，她有机会为所有美国人挺身而出。”
一些活动人士担心，她与科技公司的个人关系会缓和严肃的监管规定。例如，哈里斯第一次竞选地区检察官时的竞选经理现在经营着谷歌的加利福尼亚州政策商店。与她关系密切的姐夫托尼·韦斯特(Tony West)是优步(Uber)的总法律顾问，她的侄女直到最近还在优步工作。
哈里斯还在Facebook有人脉，这是一家这些天来民主党人怒火中烧的公司。哈里斯曾担任加州最高执法官员，多年来，她与facebook二号人物谢丽尔·桑德伯格(Sheryl Sandberg)关系特别融洽，帮助桑德伯格推销她的书《向前一步》(Lean In)。据赫芬顿邮报详细报道，桑德伯格在2016年赢得参议员席位时也给她发了贺信。
桑德伯格在这个周期中没有公开发表任何关于拜登的重要言论。但在周二，桑德伯格在Instagram上指出，哈里斯被历史性地选为第一位登上主要选票的黑人女性(尽管这位长期的民主党人没有明确支持拜登-哈里斯)。
所有这些都让人们怀疑，如果哈里斯成为副总裁，她对Facebook这样的公司是强硬还是宽松。
当马克·扎克伯格出现在国会时，哈里斯对他施加了强烈的压力。但在自己的竞标过程中，当被问及她将如何处理反垄断问题时，她含糊其辞。当被直截了当地问及科技巨头是否应该被拆分时，她闪烁其词。
她告诉“纽约时报”：“科技公司必须以我们可以确保的方式进行监管，美国消费者可以确保他们的隐私不会受到侵犯。”
她还一度试图对推特采取强硬态度，呼吁创始人杰克·多尔西(Jack Dorsey)禁止特朗普进入该平台。这并没有起到任何作用-哈里斯从那以后就退学了。
现在，如果她想要抓住机会，她又有了一次遏制硅谷的机会。
note_en:
For months, Silicon Valley hasn’t been quite sure what to make of Joe Biden.
  Biden’s selection of Harris — who has glad-handed with San Francisco elites for decades — as his choice for vice president is likely to usher in Silicon Valley excitement and money galore in a way that other running mates would not. For a top-of-the-ticket that  has struggled  until recently to excite the wealthiest and most powerful tech moguls, Harris will bring super-fans from the billionaire class that will super-charge Democrats’ coffers, especially over the next few days. Even though it makes Biden more dependent on these big donors.
 And on policy, the selection of the California senator offers some reassurance to the tech industry that has nervously watched the rise of the party’s far-left. Biden has not made tech issues a priority during the campaign, which has created uncertainty about how seriously his administration would pursue regulation or even a breakup of tech giants. With Harris — a policy pragmatist who enjoys  close relationships with many leading tech executives — Biden sends another signal that his administration will not veer toward the policies pushed by those like Elizabeth Warren, a vice-presidential short-lister that he didn’t choose who wants to  break up Big Tech.
 Cooper Teboe, a top Democratic fundraiser in Silicon Valley, said about one-third of major West Coast donors that he’s spoken to have been waiting to see who Biden would choose as vice president before deciding whether to invest tens of thousands of dollars into Democrats this cycle. Should Biden have chosen Warren, for instance, tech donors might’ve had concerns.
 “She is the safest pick for the donor community,” Teboe said of Harris. “She will be the pick that the California, Silicon Valley donor community — who are worried about things like tech and repatriation and taxes and so on and so forth — she is the pick that they will be happiest with.”
  Harris’ ties to this power set will be highlighted in just a few days’ time when she headlines a high-dollar fundraiser with a Bay Area fundraising group, Electing Women Bay Area, according to an invitation seen by Recode.
 Harris’s special touch with the ultra-rich has been integral to her  political ascent in San Francisco, where she first served for district attorney before her statewide wins as Attorney General and US Senator. Harris was a regular presence on the city’s cocktail circuit and has been profiled in society pages ever since her 30’s. Her campaigns were funded by the old-money families that predated the modern tech boom.
 When that boom did arrive, Harris capitalized and built an orbit of new-money fans that she will further bring into the Biden fold. Her biggest donors over the last two decades reads like a who’s who list of tech moguls: Salesforce founder Marc Benioff  has told Recode that Harris “one of the highest integrity people I have ever met.” Early Facebook president Sean Parker invited Harris  to his wedding. Fundraisers for her presidential bid included billionaire Democratic power brokers like Reid Hoffman and John Doerr.
 Chris Lehane, a longtime adviser to Bay Area donors, recalled Harris as a “workhorse” when it came to making fundraising calls during her first run for California attorney general in 2009.
 “She’d work the whole list,” he said, “and then ask for more names.”
 One particularly close bond for Harris has been with Democratic mega-donor Laurene Powell Jobs, the billionaire philanthropist and the wife of the late Steve Jobs. When Powell Jobs  appeared on stage to speak at the annual Code Conference in 2017, she brought Harris along with her.
 “I thought you would find it more interesting,” than having just herself, Powell Jobs remarked on stage. On Tuesday, she  tweeted that Biden had “made a great choice!”
 But all these ties will prove double-edged in a Democratic Party that has grown concerned about the wealth accumulated by these billionaires and their political influence. The same goes for their tech companies, which are now  the subject of antitrust scrutiny and a broader rethink of Silicon Valley’s corporate power.
 Roger McNamee, a Silicon Valley investor who has  expressed concerns about Biden listening too much to tech billionaires, said Harris could pull off a  “Nixon-to-China moment.” In other words, only someone like her could push through certain regulations, because of her credibility with the tech community.
 “As senator from California, Kamala Harris was understandably aligned with Big Tech,” said McNamee. “As vice president, she has an opportunity to stand up for all Americans.”
 Some  activists are concerned that her personal ties to tech companies will temper serious regulations. Harris’ campaign manager for her first race for district attorney, for instance, now runs the California state policy shop at Google. And Tony West, her brother-in-law, with whom she is close,  is the general counsel of Uber, where her niece worked until recently.
 Harris also has connections at  Facebook, a company at the burning core of Democrats’ ire these days. Harris — who served as California’s top law enforcement official — has enjoyed a particularly cozy relationship with Facebook no. 2 Sheryl Sandberg over the years, helping Sandberg market her book “Lean In.” Sandberg also sent her a congratulatory note when she won her Senate seat in 2016,  as The Huffington Post detailed.
 Sandberg hadn’t publicly said anything of significance about Biden this cycle. But then on Tuesday, Sandberg  took to Instagram to note the historic selection of Harris as the first Black woman on a major ticket (although the longtime Democrat didn’t explicitly endorse Biden-Harris).
 All of this leaves people wondering if Harris will be tough — or easy — on companies like Facebook if she becomes vice president.
 Harris strongly  pressed Mark Zuckerberg when he appeared before Congress. But she has equivocated when asked during her own bid how she would handle antitrust matters. She has dodged when asked point-blank whether the tech giants should be broken up.
 “The tech companies have got to be regulated in a way that we can ensure and the American consumer can be certain that their privacy is not being compromised,”  she told the New York Times.
 She also tried at one point to get tough on Twitter, calling on founder Jack Dorsey to ban Trump from the platform. That didn’t go anywhere — and Harris dropped out thereafter.
 Now, she has another shot at reining in Silicon Valley, if she wants to take it.
title: 拜登的副总裁人选卡马拉·哈里斯(Kamala Harris)与科技高管关系密切，并回避了拆分大型科技公司的问题，他可能会在SV引起兴奋
title_en: Biden's VP pick Kamala Harris, who has close ties with tech execs and has dodged questions about breaking up Big Tech, is likely to generate excitement in SV

跳转到导航跳转到搜索Mozilla公司(样式为moz：//a)是Mozilla基金会的全资子公司，由全球开源开发人员社区协调和集成与Internet相关的应用程序(如Firefox Web浏览器)的开发，其中一些开放源码开发人员自己受雇于该公司。该公司还分销和推广这些产品。与非营利性的Mozilla基金会和由现已停业的Netscape通信公司创建的Mozilla开源项目不同，Mozilla公司是一个应纳税的实体。Mozilla公司将其所有利润重新投资到Mozilla项目中。[4]Mozilla公司声明的目标是努力实现Mozilla基金会的公共利益，促进互联网上的选择和创新。
Mozilla基金会将最终控制Mozilla公司的活动，并将保留其对新子公司的100%所有权。Mozilla公司获得的任何利润都将重新投资到Mozilla项目中。不会有股东，不会发行股票期权，也不会支付股息。Mozilla公司将不会在股票市场上市，任何公司都不可能接管或购买该子公司的股份。Mozilla基金会将继续拥有Mozilla商标和其他知识产权，并将把它们授权给Mozilla公司。基金会还将继续管理源代码存储库，并控制允许谁签入。[6]。
Mozilla公司成立于2005年8月3日，负责处理Mozilla基金会与收入相关的业务。作为一家非营利性组织，莫兹拉基金会在收入类型和金额方面都是有限的。Mozilla公司作为一个应税组织(本质上是一种商业经营)，并不一定要遵守这样严格的规定。创建后，Mozilla公司从Mozilla基金会接管了几个领域，包括协调和整合Firefox和Thunderbird的开发(由全球自由软件社区)，以及管理与企业的关系。
随着Mozilla公司的成立，Mozilla基金会的其余成员缩小了关注范围，专注于Mozilla项目的治理和政策问题。2005年11月，随着Mozilla Firefox1.5的发布，Mozilla公司的网站mozilla.com作为Firefox和Thunderbird产品的在线新主页揭开了面纱。
2006年，Mozilla公司创造了6680万美元的收入和1980万美元的开支，其中85%的收入来自谷歌，用于将[谷歌]指定为浏览器的默认搜索引擎，以及用于在随后的搜索结果页面上放置广告的点击。
2006年3月，杰森·卡拉卡尼斯(Jason Calacanis)在他的博客上报告了一个谣言，称Mozilla公司在前一年赚了7200万美元，这主要得益于Firefox浏览器中的谷歌搜索框。[9]后来，当时的董事会成员克里斯托弗·暴雪(Christopher Blizzard)澄清了这一传言，他在博客上写道，这是不正确的，尽管没有偏离一个数量级。[10]两年后，TechCrunch写道：作为将谷歌设置为Firefox默认搜索引擎的回报，谷歌向Mozilla支付了一大笔钱-2006年，总额约为5700万美元，占该公司总收入的85%。这笔交易原定于2006年到期，但后来被延长到2008年，然后一直持续到2011年。[11]这笔交易再次延长了3年，直到2014年11月。根据协议，Mozilla将从谷歌获得另外9亿美元(每年3亿美元)，几乎是之前金额的3倍。[12]。
2006年8月，微软在Mozilla新闻组上发布了一封信[13]，并提出在其位于华盛顿州雷蒙德的总部向Mozilla软件工程师开放一个新的开源设施。作为回应，Mozilla接受了这一提议。[14]。
2014年3月，Mozilla在任命Brendan Eich为新的首席执行官(CEO)后受到了一些批评。2008年，艾奇捐赠了1000美元支持加州8号提案[15]，这是一项投票倡议，禁止加州法律承认同性婚姻。[16]据报道，6名Mozilla董事会成员中有3名因CEO的选择而辞职[17]，不过Mozilla表示，辞职的董事会成员有各种原因[18]，并重申将继续致力于LGBT平等[19][20]，包括同性婚姻。[21]4月1日，在线约会网站OkCupid开始向使用Mozilla Firefox的访问者显示一条消息，敦促他们切换到不同的网络浏览器，并指出OkCupid上8%的配对是同性伴侣之间的。[22]4月3日，Mozilla宣布
2014年4月，Mozilla前首席营销官克里斯·比尔德被任命为临时首席执行官。比尔德于同年7月28日被任命为首席执行官。[29][29]。
2017年2月27日，它收购了网络储蓄服务Pocket。[30]根据Mozilla作为默认开放模式运营的历史，以及Mozilla首席商务官Denelle Dixon-Thayer关于Pocket将成为Mozilla开源项目的一部分的评论，有报道称Pocket将成为开源。在收购之前，Pocket背后的初创公司将其作为封闭源代码的商业服务进行运营，而Mozilla将源代码作为开源向Firefox发布了添加了Save to Pocket功能的源代码。截至2019年9月，Pocket仍然是开源的，而扩展仍然是开源的。
大众媒体[31][32]已经注意到Mozilla公司与谷歌的关系，特别是关于他们的付费推荐协议。Mozilla与谷歌(Google)最初达成的让谷歌搜索(Google Search)作为浏览器默认网络搜索引擎的协议已于2011年到期，但双方达成了一项新协议，谷歌同意在三年内向Mozilla支付略低于10亿美元，以换取谷歌继续作为其默认搜索引擎。由于微软必应(Bing)和雅虎(Yahoo！)在拍卖会上的积极出价，这一价格被推高了。尽管达成了协议，Mozilla Firefox仍与必应、雅虎、Yandex、百度、亚马逊和eBay保持着合作关系。[33][33]。
特别是火狐2在2007年发布的反钓鱼保护功能引发了相当大的争议：[42]默认启用的反钓鱼保护基于谷歌服务器每小时更新两次的列表。[43]用户不能在GUI内改变数据提供者[44]，并且不被告知谁是默认数据提供者。浏览器还会在每次更新请求时发送谷歌的cookie。[45]一些[谁？]。互联网隐私倡导团体对谷歌可能使用这些数据表示担忧，特别是因为火狐的隐私政策规定，谷歌可能会与包括商业合作伙伴在内的第三方共享通过安全浏览收集的(非个人身份识别)信息。[46][中英文对照]。
2009年12月，谷歌首席执行官埃里克·施密特(Eric Schmidt)在CNBC节目中就隐私问题发表评论后，Mozilla社区发展总监阿萨·多兹勒(Asa Dotzler)建议用户使用必应(Bing)搜索引擎，而不是谷歌搜索。[48]在谷歌Chrome发布之前，谷歌还通过YouTube推广火狐。2009年8月，Mozilla Security帮助谷歌指出了谷歌Chrome浏览器的一个安全漏洞。[49][49]。
2014年11月，Mozilla与雅虎签署了为期五年的合作伙伴关系，使雅虎！在美国搜索Firefox浏览器的默认搜索引擎。[50]随着火狐Quantum于2017年11月17日发布，谷歌再次成为默认搜索引擎。[51][font=宋体]。
微软澳大利亚业务负责人史蒂夫·瓦莫斯(Steve Vamos)在2004年底表示，他不认为火狐是一种威胁，微软用户对火狐功能集的需求也不大。[52]微软董事长比尔·盖茨用过火狐，但他评论说，火狐只是另一个浏览器，IE(微软的IE浏览器)更好。[53][font=宋体]。
Microsoft SEC在2005年6月30日提交的一份文件中承认，Mozilla等竞争对手提供的软件与我们Windows操作系统产品的Internet Explorer Web浏览功能相竞争。&#34；[54]Internet Explorer 7的发布速度很快，并且包括以前在Firefox和其他浏览器中提供的功能，如选项卡式浏览和RSS提要。[55][font=宋体]
尽管受到微软最高管理层的冷遇，Internet Explorer开发团队仍与Mozilla保持着合作关系。他们定期开会讨论网络标准，例如扩展验证证书。[56]2005年，Mozilla同意允许Microsoft使用其Web提要徽标，以实现Web提要功能的通用图形表示。[57][font=宋体]。
2006年8月，微软提出帮助Mozilla将Firefox与当时即将推出的Windows Vista集成，Mozilla接受了这个提议。[59][59]。
2006年10月，为了祝贺Firefox2的成功发布，Internet Explorer7开发团队给Mozilla送去了一个蛋糕。[60]作为对浏览器大战的认可，一些人开玩笑地建议Mozilla在参考开源软件运动的同时，把蛋糕连同食谱一起寄回去。[62]2008年6月17日，在Firefox3成功发布之后，IE开发团队又送来了一块蛋糕，[63]2011年3月22日，针对Firefox4，[64]，再次针对Firefox5发布。[65][font=宋体]。
2007年11月，杰夫·琼斯(微软值得信赖的计算组的安全战略总监)批评了Firefox，声称在典型的企业场景中，Internet Explorer比Firefox经历的漏洞更少，更严重的漏洞也更少。[66]Mozilla开发者Mike Shaver对这项研究不屑一顾，称微软捆绑的安全补丁和这项研究的重点是补丁，而不是漏洞，这些都是关键缺陷。[67][67]。
2009年2月，Microsoft发布了.NET Framework 3.5版的Service Pack 1。此更新还安装了Microsoft.NET框架助手加载项(启用ClickOnce支持)。[68]在用户发现该插件无法通过插件界面卸载后，该更新受到了媒体的关注。[69]在Annoyances.org网站发布了一篇关于此更新的文章几个小时后，微软员工布拉德·艾布拉姆斯在他的博客中发布了微软为什么要安装该插件的解释，并提供了有关如何删除它的详细说明。[71]但是，消除此扩展的唯一方法是手动修改Windows注册表，如果操作不正确，这可能会导致Windows系统无法启动。[69][font=宋体]。
2009年10月16日，Mozilla阻止所有版本的Microsoft.NET框架助手与Firefox和Mozilla附加组件服务一起使用。[72]两天后，在微软确认该加载项不是漏洞传播媒介后，该加载项被从阻止列表中删除。[73]1.1版(2009年6月10日发布给Mozilla Add-ons服务)及更高版本的Microsoft.NET Framework Assistant允许用户以正常方式禁用和卸载。[75][75]。
自2010年起，火狐是向欧洲经济区用户提供的12款微软Windows浏览器之一-参见BrowserChoice.eu。[76][76]。
2008年，美国国税局(Internal Revenue Service)启动了对Mozilla基金会(Mozilla Foundation)2004-2005年收入的审计，原因是该基金会的搜索版税。2009年，调查扩大到2006和2007纳税年度，尽管这部分审计已经结束。由于Mozilla的收入中至少有三分之一不是来自公众捐款，因此它不会自动成为一家公共慈善机构。[77][font=宋体]
2012年11月，在发现Mozilla基金会欠美国国税局150万美元的和解款项后，审计结束。[78][79][79]。

Jump to navigation  Jump to search  The  Mozilla Corporation (stylized as  moz://a) is a wholly owned subsidiary of the  Mozilla Foundation that coordinates and integrates the development of  Internet-related  applications such as the  Firefox  web browser, by a global community of open-source developers, some of whom are employed by the corporation itself. The corporation also distributes and promotes these products. Unlike the  non-profit Mozilla Foundation, and the  Mozilla open source project, founded by the now defunct  Netscape Communications Corporation, the Mozilla Corporation is a  taxable entity. The Mozilla Corporation reinvests all of its profits back into the Mozilla projects.  [4] The Mozilla Corporation's stated aim is to work towards the Mozilla Foundation's  public benefit to "promote choice and innovation on the Internet."  [5]
  The Mozilla Foundation will ultimately control the activities of the Mozilla Corporation and will retain its 100 percent ownership of the new subsidiary. Any profits made by the Mozilla Corporation will be invested back into the Mozilla project. There will be no shareholders, no stock options will be issued and no dividends will be paid. The Mozilla Corporation will not be floating on the stock market and it will be impossible for any company to take over or buy a stake in the subsidiary. The Mozilla Foundation will continue to own the Mozilla trademarks and other intellectual property and will license them to the Mozilla Corporation. The Foundation will also continue to govern the source code repository and control who is allowed to check in.  [6]
   The Mozilla Corporation was established on August 3, 2005, to handle the  revenue-related operations of the Mozilla Foundation. As a non-profit, the Mozilla Foundation is limited in terms of the types and amounts of revenue. The Mozilla Corporation, as a taxable organization (essentially, a commercial operation), does not have to comply with such strict rules. Upon its creation, the Mozilla Corporation took over several areas from the Mozilla Foundation, including coordination and integration of the development of Firefox and Thunderbird (by the global  free software community) and the management of relationships with  businesses.
 With the creation of the Mozilla Corporation, the rest of the Mozilla Foundation narrowed its focus to concentrate on the Mozilla project's governance and policy issues. In November 2005, with the release of Mozilla Firefox 1.5, the Mozilla Corporation's website at mozilla.com was unveiled as the new home of the Firefox and Thunderbird products online.
 In 2006, the Mozilla Corporation generated $66.8 million in revenue and $19.8 million in expenses, with 85% of that revenue coming from Google for "assigning [Google] as the browser's default search engine, and for click-throughs on ads placed on the ensuing search results pages."  [7]  [8]
  In March 2006,  Jason Calacanis reported a rumor on his blog that Mozilla Corporation gained $72M during the previous year, mainly thanks to the  Google search box in the Firefox browser.  [9] The rumor was later addressed by  Christopher Blizzard, then a member of the board, who wrote on his blog that, "it’s not correct, though not off by an order of magnitude."  [10] Two years later,   TechCrunch wrote: "In return for setting Google as the default search engine on Firefox, Google pays Mozilla a substantial sum – in 2006, the total amounted to around $57 million, or 85% of the company’s total revenue. The deal was originally going to expire in 2006, but was later extended to 2008 and then ran through 2011."  [11] The deal was extended again another 3 years, until November 2014. Under the deal, Mozilla was to have received from Google another $900 million ($300 million annually), nearly 3 times the previous amount.  [12]
 In August 2006,  Microsoft posted a letter on Mozilla newsgroups  [13] and offered to open up a new open-source facility at its headquarters in Redmond, Wash., to Mozilla software engineers. Mozilla responded by accepting the offer.  [14]
 In March 2014, Mozilla came under some criticism after it appointed  Brendan Eich as its new Chief Executive Officer (CEO). In 2008, Eich had made a $1,000 contribution in support of  California Proposition 8,  [15] a ballot initiative that barred legal recognition of  same-sex marriages in California.  [16] Three of six Mozilla board members reportedly resigned over the choice of CEO,  [17] though Mozilla said the resigning board members had "a variety of reasons"  [18] and reasserted its continued commitment to LGBT equality,  [19]  [20] including same-sex marriage.  [21] On April 1, the  online dating site  OkCupid started displaying visitors using  Mozilla Firefox a message urging them to switch to a different web browser, pointing out that 8% of the matches made on OkCupid are between same-sex couples.  [22] On April 3, Mozilla announced that Eich had decided to step down as CEO and also leave the board of Mozilla Foundation.  [23]  [24] This, in turn, prompted criticism from some commentators who criticized the pressure that led Eich to resign.  [25]  [26]  [27] For example,  Conor Friedersdorf argued in   The Atlantic that "the general practice of punishing people in business for bygone political donations is most likely to entrench powerful interests and weaken the ability of the powerless to challenge the status quo."  [28]
 In April 2014,  Chris Beard, the former chief marketing officer of Mozilla, was appointed interim CEO. Beard was named CEO in July 28 of the same year.  [29]
 On February 27, 2017, it acquired the web saving service  Pocket.  [30] In accordance with Mozilla's history of operating as " open by default" and based on comments by Mozilla chief business officer Denelle Dixon-Thayer that Pocket would "become part of the Mozilla open source project", it was reported that Pocket would become open source. Prior to the acquisition, the startup behind Pocket operated it as a closed source, commercial service, and Mozilla published the source code that added a "Save to Pocket" feature to Firefox as open source. As of September 2019  Pocket remains closed source, while the extension remains open source.
    The Mozilla Corporation's relationship with  Google has been noted in the popular press,  [31]  [32] especially with regard to their paid referral agreement. Mozilla's original deal with Google to have  Google Search as the default  web search engine in the browser expired in 2011, but a new deal was struck, where Google agreed to pay Mozilla just under a billion dollars over three years in exchange for keeping Google as its default search engine. The price was driven up due to aggressive bidding from Microsoft's  Bing and  Yahoo!'s presence in the auction as well. Despite the deal, Mozilla Firefox maintains relationships with Bing, Yahoo!,  Yandex,  Baidu,  Amazon.com and  eBay.  [33]
  The 2007 release of the anti- phishing protection in Firefox 2 in particular raised considerable controversy:  [42] Anti-phishing protection, enabled by default, is based on a list updated twice hourly from Google's servers.  [43] The user cannot change the data provider within the GUI,  [44] and is not informed who the default data provider is. The browser also sends Google's  cookie with each update request.  [45] Some [   who?]  internet privacy advocacy groups have expressed concerns surrounding Google's possible uses for this data, especially since Firefox's privacy policy states that Google may share (non-personally identifying) information gathered through safe browsing with third parties, including business partners.  [46]
 Following Google CEO  Eric Schmidt's comments in December 2009 regarding privacy during a  CNBC show,  [47] Asa Dotzler, Mozilla's director of community development suggested that users use the  Bing search engine instead of Google search.  [48] Google also promoted Firefox through YouTube until the release of  Google Chrome. In August 2009, Mozilla Security assisted Google by pointing out a security flaw in Google's Chrome browser.  [49]
  In November 2014, Mozilla signed a five-year partnership with Yahoo!, making  Yahoo! Search the default search engine for Firefox browsers in the US.  [50] With the release of Firefox Quantum on November 17, 2017, Google became the default search engine again.  [51]
  Microsoft's head of Australian operations, Steve Vamos, stated in late 2004 that he did not see Firefox as a threat and that there was not significant demand for the feature-set of Firefox among Microsoft's users.  [52] Microsoft Chairman  Bill Gates has used Firefox, but has commented that "it's just another browser, and IE [Microsoft's Internet Explorer] is better".  [53]
 A Microsoft  SEC filing on June 30, 2005 acknowledged that "competitors such as Mozilla offer software that competes with the Internet Explorer Web browsing capabilities of our Windows operating system products."  [54] The release of Internet Explorer 7 was fast tracked, and included functionality that was previously available in Firefox and other browsers, such as tabbed browsing and RSS feeds.  [55]
 Despite the cold reception from Microsoft's top management, the Internet Explorer development team maintains a relationship with Mozilla. They meet regularly to discuss web standards such as  extended validation certificates.  [56] In 2005, Mozilla agreed to allow Microsoft to use its  Web feed logo in the interest of common graphical representation of the Web feeds feature.  [57]
 In August 2006, Microsoft offered to help Mozilla integrate Firefox with the then-forthcoming  Windows Vista,  [58] an offer Mozilla accepted.  [59]
 In October 2006, as congratulations for a successful ship of Firefox 2, the Internet Explorer 7 development team sent a cake to Mozilla.  [60]  [61] As a nod to the  browser wars, some jokingly suggested that Mozilla send a cake back along with the recipe, in reference to the  open-source software movement.  [62] The IE development team sent another cake on June 17, 2008, upon the successful release of Firefox 3,  [63] again on March 22, 2011, for Firefox 4,  [64] and yet again for the Firefox 5 release.  [65]
 In November 2007, Jeff Jones (a "security strategy director" in Microsoft's Trustworthy Computing Group) criticized Firefox, claiming that Internet Explorer experienced fewer  vulnerabilities and fewer higher severity vulnerabilities than Firefox in typical enterprise scenarios.  [66]Mozilla developer  Mike Shaver discounted the study, citing Microsoft's bundling of security fixes and the study's focus on fixes, rather than vulnerabilities, as crucial flaws.  [67]
 In February 2009, Microsoft released Service Pack 1 for  version 3.5 of the .NET Framework. This update also installed Microsoft .NET Framework Assistant add-on (enabling  ClickOnce support).  [68] The update received media attention after users discovered that the add-on could not be uninstalled through the add-ons interface.  [69]  [70] Several hours after the website Annoyances.org posted an article regarding this update, Microsoft employee Brad Abrams posted in his blog Microsoft's explanation for why the add-on was installed, and also included detailed instructions on how to remove it.  [71] However, the only way to get rid of this extension was to modify manually the  Windows Registry, which could cause Windows systems to fail to boot up if not done correctly.  [69]
 On October 16, 2009, Mozilla blocked all versions of Microsoft .NET Framework Assistant from being used with Firefox and from the  Mozilla Add-ons service.  [72] Two days later, the add-on was removed from the blocklist after confirmation from Microsoft that it is not a vector for  vulnerabilities.  [73]  [74] Version 1.1 (released on June 10, 2009 to the Mozilla Add-ons service) and later of the Microsoft .NET Framework Assistant allows the user to disable and uninstall in the normal fashion.  [75]
 Firefox was one of the twelve browsers offered to  European Economic Area users of  Microsoft Windows from 2010 – see  BrowserChoice.eu.  [76]
  The  Internal Revenue Service opened an audit of the  Mozilla Foundation's 2004-5 revenues in 2008, due to its search royalties, and in 2009, the investigation was expanded to the 2006 and 2007 tax years, though that part of the audit was closed. As Mozilla does not derive at least a third of its revenue from public donations, it does not automatically qualify as a public charity.  [77]
 In November 2012, the audit was closed after finding that the Mozilla Foundation owed a settlement of $1.5 million to the IRS.  [78]  [79]
    The  board of directors is appointed by and responsible to  Mozilla Foundation's board. In March 2014, half the board members resigned.  [17] The remaining board members are:
        Ellen Siminoff, former board member, also President and CEO of Shmoop University and Chairman of  Efficient Frontier

Simpplr今天为其人工智能驱动的内联网平台筹集了1000万美元，该平台旨在简化通信。首席执行官Dhiraj Sharma表示，随着Simpplr开发新产品并扩大其合作伙伴生态系统，这些资金将用于推动客户在疫情期间的采用。
随着越来越多的人在家工作，企业重新将重点放在内部网上，以便远程联系和管理员工。(“内联网”是指公司的内部网络，用于信息共享和协作工具。)。但内部网传统上存在参与度问题。根据一项有先见之明的数字媒体调查，只有13%的员工报告每天使用他们的内部网。
Simpplr声称其人工智能技术通过根据角色、位置、任期、部门、主题兴趣和以前的搜索定制用户体验，确保内联网内容是最新的、相关的和引人注目的。端到端加密平台与Box、Google Drive、Slake和Microsoft Office 365等数字工作空间集成，允许员工从原生Android和iOS应用程序或网络中选择访问公司门户。
Simpplr有一个员工名录，上面有个人简介，包括传记、专业领域、活动、社会关系和其他专业花絮。通过受Facebook启发的订阅源，员工可以通过共享、点赞和回复内容或关注用户并参与投票、测验和调查来与其他员工互动。他们还可以关注和喜爱某些同事，以了解内部帖子、博客、照片、日历事件、视频、公告和庆祝活动。
管理者可以在Simpplr中为团队、部门和社区创建社交工作区，他们还可以使用完全可搜索的预格式化页面添加知识库。特权用户可以查看分析，以了解哪些内容在浏览量和参与度方面表现最好，并根据角色、位置和域个性化员工体验，同时允许他们自定义和组织其主页。
Simpplr的自动治理引擎可以自动监控、显示和取消发布陈旧内容，并且可以审计活动或跟踪高需求的内容。它还提醒作者在内容需要刷新时采取行动，并促进审查、验证和归档过程，从其他系统提取最新内容，同时提供某些内容未被阅读的原因分析。
管理员可以使用Simpplr发布电子邮件摘要和时事通讯，或者将一篇文章标记为“强制阅读”。与其他地方不同的是，Simpplr提供了一个搜索工具，可以快速访问人员、文件、网站等；提供一个品牌编辑器，可以自定义颜色、字体和徽标；以及一个访问控制工具，可以管理角色和用户权限，以定义经理和员工可以采取的操作。
尽管面临Workvivo和Happeo等公司的竞争，Simpplr声称，包括AAA、福克斯、Vertex制药、DocuSign、世界经济论坛和哥伦比亚大学在内的200个组织的25万人使用其平台。(该公司去年的年度经常性收入增长了130%。)。在Workday，Simpplr的推荐算法将支持请求减少了52%。而在欧洲之星(Eurostar)和未来之州(Future State)，他们表面上提高了员工敬业度，并将员工入职沟通分别减少了253%和25%。
今天宣布的B系列融资由Norwest Venture Partners牵头，Salesforce Ventures公司参与，使Simpplr筹集的资金总额达到近3000万美元。这家成立六年的公司总部设在加利福尼亚州红杉城，在印度古尔冈设有办事处。
note_en:
Simpplr today raised $10 million for its AI-powered intranet platform that is designed to streamline communications. CEO Dhiraj Sharma says the funds will be used to drive customer adoption during the pandemic as Simpplr develops new products and extends its partner ecosystem.
 With people increasingly work from home, businesses have put a renewed focus on intranets to engage with and manage employees remotely. (“Intranet” refers to a company’s internal network for information sharing and collaboration tools.) But intranets have traditionally had an engagement problem. According to a Prescient Digital Media  survey, only 13% of workers report using their intranets on a daily basis.
  Simpplr claims its AI technologies ensure intranet content is up-to-date, relevant, and compelling by tailoring users’ experiences based on role, location, tenure, department, topical interests, and previous searches. The end-to-end encrypted platform integrates with digital workspaces like Box, Google Drive, Slack, and Microsoft Office 365, allowing employees to choose from native Android and iOS apps or the web to access their company’s portal.
 Simpplr features an employee directory with profiles incorporating biographies, areas of expertise, activities, social connections, and other professional tidbits. From a Facebook-inspired feed, employees can engage with other employees by sharing, liking, and responding to content or by following users and participating in polls, quizzes, and surveys. They can also follow and favorite certain coworkers to keep up with internal posts, blogs, photos, calendar events, videos, announcements, and celebrations.
 Managers can create social workspaces within Simpplr for teams, departments, and communities, and they can add knowledge bases using preformatted pages that remain fully searchable. Privileged users can view analytics to see which content is performing best in terms of views and engagement and personalize employees’ experiences based on role, location, and domain while allowing them to customize and organize their home page.
  Simpplr’s auto-governance engine automatically monitors, surfaces, and unpublishes stale content and can audit activities or track content that’s in high demand. It also alerts authors to act when content needs a refresh and facilitates review, validation, and archival processes, pulling the latest content from other systems while offering analytics on why certain content isn’t being read.
 Admins can publish email digests and newsletters using Simpplr or mark an article a “mandatory read.”  Elsewhere, Simpplr offers a search tool that provides quick access to people, files, sites, and more; a brand editor that customizes colors, fonts, and logos; and an access control tool that manages roles and user permissions to define what actions managers and employees can take.
 Despite competition from the likes of  Workvivo and  Happeo, Simpplr claims that 250,000 people across 200 organizations — including AAA, Fox, Vertex Pharmaceuticals, DocuSign, the World Economic Forum, and Columbia University — use its platform. (The company’s annual recurring revenue grew 130% last year.) At Workday, Simpplr’s recommendation algorithms decreased support requests by 52%. And at Eurostar and Future State, they ostensibly boosted employee engagement and decreased employee onboarding communication by 253% and 25%, respectively.
 The series B round announced today was led by Norwest Venture Partners, with participation from Salesforce Ventures, and brings Simpplr’s total raised to nearly $30 million. The six-year-old company is headquartered in Redwood City, California, with an office in Gurgaon, India.
